ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general squid food product class (FOODON_03302051); no specific entry for grilled squid with sambal marinade exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW covers squid and cuttlefish as a category; no specific grilled-with-sambal preparation code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for grilled squid dish; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of sotong panggang is approximately 150g, equivalent to one medium-sized whole grilled squid with sambal marinade, as commonly served at Singaporean hawker centres and Malay food stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Sotong Panggang — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sotong Panggang (cr_id F03060032). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central grilled squid entry (NDB 15149) as approximately 1.5g/100g and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for sotong panggang. GI estimated at approximately 30 (low) based on analogous grilled seafood and squid preparations from the University of Sydney GI Database. Squid and seafood dishes with minimal carbohydrate content (3.3g/100g) and high protein (14.2g/100g) consistently yield low GI values. Glycemic load per 150g serving calculated as: (30 × 3.3 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.49, rounded to 1.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 541mg/100g — elevated; belacan (shrimp paste) marinade likely increases actual sodium further
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit sotong panggang to a small portion (80–100g) per meal, request reduced belacan and salt in the sambal marinade, and avoid pairing with other high-sodium dishes; the potassium content (280mg/100g) provides partial offset but does not neutralise the sodium burden at typical serving sizes.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (3.3g/100g), low sugar (2.4g/100g), high protein (14.2g/100g), and meaningful dietary fibre (3.3g/100g) support glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sotong panggang is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate and sugar content, high protein, and low estimated GI (~30); avoid pairing with large portions of white rice and monitor for added sugar in commercial sambal preparations.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (541mg/100g) is a cardiovascular risk factor; fat content is moderate (5.63g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~1.5g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, sotong panggang offers lean protein and low saturated fat, which are favourable, but the high sodium content from belacan and sambal marinade warrants portion control (≤100g) and preference for home-prepared versions with reduced salt and shrimp paste.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 541mg/100g exceeds 400mg/100g RA threshold; chili peppers in sambal are nightshade ingredients that may trigger solanine-related joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should exercise caution with sotong panggang due to elevated sodium and the presence of nightshade ingredients (chili peppers) in the sambal marinade, which may aggravate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals; request reduced chili and belacan and limit portion size.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sotong panggang is a popular Singaporean and Malaysian grilled squid dish in which whole or cleaned squid is marinated in a spiced sambal paste — typically comprising chili, belacan (fermented shrimp paste), lemongrass, garlic, shallots, and lime juice — before being grilled over charcoal or an open flame. The dish is a staple at Singaporean hawker centres, Malay food stalls, and pasar malam (night markets). The grilling process imparts a smoky, slightly charred exterior while the sambal marinade delivers a complex flavour profile of spicy, savoury, and mildly tangy notes. The texture of the squid is tender and slightly chewy when properly cooked. Nutritionally, sotong panggang provides 121 kcal per 100g, with high protein (14.2g), moderate fat (5.63g), low carbohydrate (3.3g), and meaningful dietary fibre (3.3g) from the sambal ingredients. Sodium is elevated at 541mg/100g due to belacan, making it a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The dish is suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions without high-carbohydrate accompaniments. The estimated glycemic index is low (~30), reflecting the minimal carbohydrate content and high protein composition.
common_user_queries: Is sotong panggang suitable for diabetics?
Is sotong panggang high in sodium?
What is sotong panggang made of?

ingredient_synonyms.belacan: shrimp paste, fermented prawn paste, blachan, blacan
ingredient_synonyms.sambal: chili paste, spiced chili sauce, Malay chili condiment

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F03060032) with confidence 0.95. This is an exact match to the canonical HPB entry for sotong panggang. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central grilled squid data as a secondary supplement. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database methodology for low-carbohydrate seafood dishes.
notes: Saturated fat (1.5g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central grilled squid entry (NDB 15149) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item; this is documented as an adapted value. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid profile data for this specific sotong panggang preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Potassium value of 280mg/100g is retained from the input record's health suitability reasoning as a plausible estimate consistent with squid nutritional profiles in USDA data; it is not directly reported in the HPB entry and should be verified. The dietary_fibre_g value of 3.3g/100g is sourced from HPB and reflects fibre contributed by sambal ingredients (chili, lemongrass, shallots). The keto dietary tag from the input is retained as defensible given carbohydrate content of 3.3g/100g. Nightshade caution for rheumatoid arthritis is preserved from the input record's clinical reasoning. Ontology match types set to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 as no exact grilled-squid-with-sambal entry exists in either ontology system.
